content.problemStatement:
Consider a grid with HHH rows (numbered 111 through HHH) and WWW columns (numbered 111 through WWW). Let's denote the value in a cell in row iii and column jjj by hi,jh_{i,j}hi,j​. NNN particles (numbered 111 through NNN) are distributed in the cells in such a way that there is at most one particle in each cell. For each valid iii, the iii-th particle has a value pip_ipi​ and it is placed in the cell in row yiy_iyi​ and column xix_ixi​.

Let's denote the set of all particles by PPP and the set of all unordered pairs of particles that are located in side-adjacent cells by EEE. We define the value of the grid as
V=∑i∈Ppi⋅hyi,xi+∑(i,j)∈Epi⋅pj .V = \sum_{i \in P} p_i \cdot h_{y_i,x_i} + \sum_{(i,j) \in E} p_i \cdot p_j \,.V=∑i∈P​pi​⋅hyi​,xi​​+∑(i,j)∈E​pi​⋅pj​.

You are given the grid and the locations of all particles, but not their exact values ― only ∣p1∣,∣p2∣,…,∣pN∣\lvert p_1 \rvert, \lvert p_2 \rvert, \ldots, \lvert p_N \rvert∣p1​∣,∣p2​∣,…,∣pN​∣. Your task is to choose the sign of each value pip_ipi​ in such a way that the value VVV is maximum possible. Each sign may be chosen independently from all other signs.
constraints: ∣hi,j∣≤1,000\lvert h_{i,j} \rvert \le 1,000∣hi,j​∣≤1,000 for each valid i,ji, ji,j
1≤yi≤H1 \le y_i \le H1≤yi​≤H for each valid iii
1≤xi≤W1 \le x_i \le W1≤xi​≤W for each valid iii
no two particles are located in the same cell
the sum of H⋅WH \cdot WH⋅W over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers HHH, WWW and NNN.
HHH lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains WWW space-separated integers hi,1,hi,2,…,hi,Wh_{i,1}, h_{i,2}, \ldots, h_{i,W}hi,1​,hi,2​,…,hi,W​.
NNN more lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains three space-separated integers yiy_iyi​, xix_ixi​ and ∣pi∣\lvert p_i \rvert∣pi​∣.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print two lines.


The first of these lines should contain a single integer ― the maximum value of VVV.
The second line should contain NNN space-separated integers. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these integers should be 111 if you choose a positive sign for the value of the iii-th particle (i.e. its value should be ∣pi∣\lvert p_i \rvert∣pi​∣) or −1-1−1 if you choose a negative sign (i.e. its value should be −∣pi∣-\lvert p_i \rvert−∣pi​∣).
If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one.
